intro.title: Four Steps to AI-Assisted Diagnosis
intro.desc: Complete the four steps below. The system synthesizes observation, palpation, and inquiry to generate a personalized pattern report.

info.label: Patient Info
info.title: Step 1 · Basic Information
info.desc: Provide the following details to inform constitution and pattern analysis.

tongue.label: Tongue Image
tongue.title: Step 2 · Capture Tongue Image
tongue.desc: Follow the photo guidance below and upload a clear tongue image.

pulse.label: Pulse Wave
pulse.title: Step 3 · Capture Pulse Wave
pulse.desc: Wear the external pulse-diagnosis device, stay still, and begin capture.

diagnose.label: AI Diagnosis
diagnose.title: Step 4 · Begin AI Diagnosis
diagnose.desc: Confirm the information above, then start the AI pattern analysis.

tips: Shoot under natural light. Avoid strong yellow lighting, shadows, and flash.
Avoid coloring foods (coffee, juice, dragon fruit, etc.) for 30 minutes before. Do not scrape the tongue coating.
Face the camera directly. Extend the tongue naturally with the tip pointing down; do not strain.
Frame the entire tongue surface — tip, middle, root, and both edges.
Hold the camera 15–20 cm away. Ensure sharp focus and no glare.
tongue.upload_hint: Click or drag image here · JPG / PNG supported

pulse.unsupported: Your browser does not support the Web Serial API. Use Chrome, Edge or another Chromium-based browser over https:// or localhost.
guidance: Sit quietly for about 5 minutes to calm the breath.
Wear the device on the left wrist at the cun-kou (radial artery) position.
Do not move your arm or speak during capture.
Capture for at least 30 seconds for a stable waveform.

diagnose.analyzing: AI is analyzing your pattern, please wait…
diagnose.result_pattern_value: Qi and Yin Deficiency · Liver Qi Stagnation with Spleen Deficiency (sample)
diagnose.result_advice_value: Soothe the liver, strengthen the spleen, supplement Qi and nourish Yin. Keep a regular schedule, eat lightly, exercise moderately. Reference herbal tea: Tai Zi Shen, Mai Dong, Chen Pi.
diagnose.disclaimer: ※ This report is for reference only. Please consult a licensed TCM practitioner for diagnosis.
